authority_boundary.fixed_between_variants: 6.5 m spacing, 24 m river setback, project-owned eight-form coigue meshes, V12 runtime-far representation, V22 RGB-padded leaf atlas, alpha2 no-shadow material, source masks, terrain, water, rocks, cameras, lighting, physics, and saved map
authority_boundary.changed_in_world_stable_variant: a corridor-anchored 6.0 km by 2.2 km curvilinear centerline domain and 24000-tree target replace each camera's 2.2 km square and 9000-tree target; a 512-cell chamfer signed-distance occupancy field with 180 m feather replaces scalar source-mask luma feathering

accepted_findings: The corrected curvilinear domain follows the river bend and supplies visible canopy in both guide-seat and river-eye frames; the rejected initial straight-domain capture was overwritten and is not retained as evidence.

Both world-stable runs evaluate the same 148909 candidates, accept the same 24000 transforms, record identical rejection counts and minimum river distance, and produce the same f30715fa74aee851 placement fingerprint.
The world-stable coordinate contract separates forest identity from camera position while still selecting near, mid, and runtime-far representations independently for each viewpoint.
The 512-cell signed-distance field is a reusable deterministic mechanism for spatial mask treatment, even though the current 180 m feather and density response fail the visual gate.
rejection_reasons: The 180 m spatial feather adds scattered edge patches and diagonal contour traces without removing the dominant hard forest bands inherited from source occupancy and coarse terrain.
Guide-seat dark coverage improves while river-eye dark coverage regresses, so the settings do not provide a consistent photometric or compositional gain across the corridor.
Only 998 guide-seat and 1290 river-eye trees use near or mid representations; most of the 24000-tree workload is runtime-far and has not been profiled as streamed world cells from a moving boat.
A single repeated coigue family cannot provide age, crown, species, riparian, understory, epiphyte, snag, or deadwood diversity and still exposes uniform trunk walls at stand edges.
Smooth 30 m terrain, tan provisional ground, flat tiled water, sparse boulder and gravel structure, clear atmosphere, and absent mist or spray remain dominant scene failures.
next_iteration_requirements: Keep the curvilinear world coordinate and fingerprint contract, but retune occupancy with narrower ecology-reviewed transitions, stronger world-space edge breakup, and explicit treeline, aspect, elevation, and river-distance controls.
Partition accepted transforms into deterministic streamed world cells and prove identity, culling, and no popping across a moving guide-seat camera before increasing density.
Add ecology-reviewed age and vertical strata: coigue adults and intermediates, a second accepted native canopy family, riparian shrubs, bamboo or fern understory, epiphyte cues, snags, and deadwood.

Pair the next ecology pass with world-aligned terrain detail, source-consistent forest-floor color, improved turquoise water response, boulder and gravel-bar structure, humid mountain atmosphere, valley cloud, and spray.
Profile HISM culling, masked overdraw, memory, streaming, and packaged desktop, console, handheld, and VR cost before production promotion.
